questions.stem: A company has a temporary difference that will result in taxable amounts in future years. This creates a:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Deferred tax asset

choices.label: B
choices.text: Deferred tax liability

choices.label: C
choices.text: Current tax payable

choices.label: D
choices.text: Permanent difference

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-25-20, a deferred tax liability is recognized for temporary differences that will result in taxable amounts in future years (i.e., the tax basis of the asset or liability is less than its reported amount, leading to future taxable income when the difference reverses).
questions.explanationJa: 将来の課税所得を生じさせる一時差異(将来加算一時差異)に対して繰延税金負債(DTL)を認識する。すなわち、資産の帳簿価額が税務基準額を上回る場合、差異の解消時に将来の課税所得が生じるため、DTLが発生する。

questions.stem: Under ASC 740, when should a valuation allowance be established for a deferred tax asset?

choices.label: A
choices.text: When it is probable that all of the deferred tax asset will not be realized

choices.label: B
choices.text: When it is more likely than not that some portion of the deferred tax asset will not be realized

choices.label: C
choices.text: When there is a reasonable possibility the asset will not be realized

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only when the entity has cumulative losses in the past five years

questions.explanation: ASC 740-10-30-5 states that a valuation allowance shall be recognized if, based on the weight of available evidence, it is more likely than not (a likelihood of more than 50 percent) that some portion or all of the deferred tax asset will not be realized.
questions.explanationJa: 入手可能な証拠の重みに基づき、繰延税金資産(DTA)の一部または全部が実現しない可能性が50%超(more likely than not)である場合、評価性引当額(valuation allowance)を認識しなければならない。

questions.stem: Under intraperiod tax allocation, which of the following items would NOT have income tax allocated to it?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Income from continuing operations

choices.label: B
choices.text: Discontinued operations

choices.label: C
choices.text: Other comprehensive income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Cash dividends declared

questions.explanation: Intraperiod tax allocation requires that the total income tax expense for a period be allocated among: continuing operations, discontinued operations, other comprehensive income, and items charged or credited directly to equity. Cash dividends declared are a distribution of earnings, not an income item, and do not require tax allocation.
questions.explanationJa: 期間内税金配分(intraperiod tax allocation)では、期間の法人税等合計額を継続事業、非継続事業、その他の包括利益、資本に直接計上される項目に配分する。現金配当の宣言は利益の分配であり収益項目ではないため、税金配分の対象外である。

questions.stem: Under ASC 740-10, what is the recognition threshold for a tax position to be recognized in the financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: More likely than not (greater than 50%)

choices.label: B
choices.text: Probable (greater than 75%)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reasonably possible (greater than slight)

choices.label: D
choices.text: Virtually certain (greater than 95%)

questions.explanation: ASC 740-10-25-6 requires a two-step process: first, the tax position must meet the more-likely-than-not recognition threshold (greater than 50% likelihood of being sustained upon examination). If it meets the threshold, it is then measured at the largest amount of benefit that is greater than 50% likely of being realized upon settlement.
questions.explanationJa: 不確実な税務ポジションには2段階のプロセスが求められる。まず、税務ポジションが「more likely than not」の認識閾値(税務調査で維持される可能性が50%超)を満たす必要がある。閾値を満たした場合、決済時に実現する可能性が50%超である最大の便益額で測定する。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 2, the enacted tax rate changes from 30% to 25%. Entity G has a deferred tax liability of $60,000 measured at the old rate (representing $200,000 of temporary differences). What adjustment should Entity G record?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Decrease the deferred tax liability by $10,000 and recognize a $10,000 benefit

choices.label: B
choices.text: No adjustment until the temporary differences reverse

choices.label: C
choices.text: Increase the deferred tax liability by $10,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Decrease the deferred tax liability by $10,000 and credit other comprehensive income

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-35-4, deferred tax assets and liabilities are adjusted when there is a change in tax laws or rates. The new liability is $200,000 × 25% = $50,000. The decrease of $10,000 ($60,000 - $50,000) is recognized in income tax expense (benefit) in the period that includes the enactment date.
questions.explanationJa: 税率の変更があった場合、繰延税金資産・負債は新税率で再測定する。新DTL = $200,000 × 25% = $50,000。減少額$10,000($60,000 - $50,000)は、法律の制定日を含む期間に法人税等ベネフィットとして認識する。

questions.stem: Under current tax law (post-TCJA), net operating losses (NOLs) generated after December 31, 2017 can be carried forward:

choices.label: A
choices.text: For 20 years and can offset 100% of taxable income

choices.label: B
choices.text: Indefinitely but are limited to 80% of taxable income

choices.label: C
choices.text: For 5 years with no limitation

choices.label: D
choices.text: Indefinitely with no limitation

questions.explanation: The Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (TCJA) of 2017 changed NOL rules for losses arising after December 31, 2017. These NOLs can be carried forward indefinitely (no expiration) but are limited to offsetting 80% of taxable income in any given year.
questions.explanationJa: 2017年の税制改革法(TCJA)により、2017年12月31日以降に発生したNOLのルールが変更された。これらのNOLは無期限に繰り越せるが、各年度の課税所得の80%までしか相殺できない。

questions.stem: Which of the following is a permanent difference that will never result in a deferred tax asset or liability?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Depreciation differences between book and tax

choices.label: B
choices.text: Warranty expense accrued for book but deducted when paid for tax

choices.label: C
choices.text: Municipal bond interest income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Rent received in advance

questions.explanation: Municipal bond interest is exempt from federal income tax and is never included in taxable income. This creates a permanent difference because the difference between book income and taxable income will never reverse. Permanent differences affect the effective tax rate but do not create deferred tax assets or liabilities.
questions.explanationJa: 地方債利息は連邦所得税が免除されており、課税所得に含まれない。帳簿上の利益と課税所得の差異が永久に解消されないため、永久差異(permanent difference)となる。永久差異は実効税率に影響するが、繰延税金資産・負債は生じさせない。

questions.stem: Entity H has equipment with a book carrying value of $500,000 and a tax basis of $350,000. The enacted tax rate is 21%. What deferred tax amount should be reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Deferred tax liability of $31,500

choices.label: B
choices.text: Deferred tax asset of $31,500

choices.label: C
choices.text: Deferred tax liability of $150,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Deferred tax asset of $150,000

questions.explanation: Under the balance sheet approach (ASC 740-10-25-20), a deferred tax liability arises when the book carrying value exceeds the tax basis (indicating future taxable amounts). The temporary difference is $500,000 - $350,000 = $150,000. The deferred tax liability = $150,000 × 21% = $31,500.
questions.explanationJa: 貸借対照表アプローチでは、帳簿価額が税務基準額を超える場合(将来の課税額を示す)、繰延税金負債(DTL)が発生する。一時差異 = $500,000 - $350,000 = $150,000。DTL = $150,000 × 21% = $31,500。

questions.stem: Under ASC 740, a deferred tax liability is generally NOT recognized for the excess of financial reporting over tax basis of an investment in a domestic subsidiary when:

choices.label: A
choices.text: The parent has the ability and intent to recover the investment tax-free

choices.label: B
choices.text: The subsidiary has cumulative losses

choices.label: C
choices.text: The parent can demonstrate indefinite reversal

choices.label: D
choices.text: The difference is related to undistributed earnings that are indefinitely reinvested

questions.explanation: ASC 740-30-25-17 provides an exception: a deferred tax liability is not recognized for undistributed earnings of a domestic subsidiary when the tax law provides a means by which the investment can be recovered tax-free and the parent has the intent and ability to use that means.
questions.explanationJa: 例外規定がある。税法が投資を非課税で回収する手段を提供し、親会社がその手段を利用する意図と能力を有する場合、国内子会社の未分配利益に対する繰延税金負債は認識しない。実質的には無期限に再投資される利益について、DTLの認識が不要となる。

questions.stem: Entity J has pretax income of $1,000,000 and total income tax expense of $250,000. The statutory rate is 21%. Which of the following could explain why the effective rate (25%) is higher than the statutory rate?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Tax-exempt municipal bond interest

choices.label: B
choices.text: Nondeductible meals and entertainment expenses

choices.label: C
choices.text: Research and development tax credits

choices.label: D
choices.text: Excess tax benefits from stock compensation

questions.explanation: Nondeductible expenses (such as certain meals and entertainment, fines, and penalties) increase the effective tax rate above the statutory rate because they are included in book income but not deductible for tax purposes. Tax-exempt income, tax credits, and excess tax benefits all reduce the effective rate.
questions.explanationJa: 損金不算入費用(特定の飲食・接待費、罰金、違約金など)は帳簿上の利益に含まれるが税務上は損金算入できないため、実効税率を法定税率より引き上げる。非課税所得、税額控除、株式報酬の超過税額便益はいずれも実効税率を引き下げる。

questions.stem: Alpha Corp. has pretax financial income of $800,000. This includes $20,000 of tax-exempt municipal bond interest (permanent difference). Additionally, tax depreciation exceeds book depreciation by $50,000 (temporary difference). The enacted tax rate is 25%. What is total income tax expense?
questions.explanation: Total income tax expense = (Pretax income - Permanent differences) × Tax rate. Temporary differences shift between current and deferred but do not affect total expense. Total expense = ($800,000 - $20,000) × 25% = $780,000 × 25% = $195,000. Current portion = ($800,000 - $20,000 - $50,000) × 25% = $182,500. Deferred portion = $50,000 × 25% = $12,500.

questions.stem: Beta Corp. has the following temporary differences at year-end: Warranty liability (book basis $100,000, tax basis $0) creating a future deductible amount; Equipment (book basis $800,000, tax basis $600,000) creating a future taxable amount of $200,000. The enacted tax rate is 21%. What is the net deferred tax liability?
questions.explanation: Warranty liability creates a DTA (future deductible): $100,000 × 21% = $21,000. Equipment creates a DTL (future taxable): $200,000 × 21% = $42,000. Net deferred tax = DTL $42,000 - DTA $21,000 = $21,000 net DTL.
questions.explanationJa: 製品保証引当金はDTA(将来減算一時差異)を生じさせる: $100,000 × 21% = $21,000。設備はDTL(将来加算一時差異)を生じさせる: $200,000 × 21% = $42,000。繰延税金純額 = DTL $42,000 - DTA $21,000 = $21,000の繰延税金負債(純額)。

questions.stem: Gamma Corp. has a gross deferred tax asset of $150,000. Based on available evidence, management concludes that it is more likely than not that only $90,000 of the DTA will be realized. What is the net deferred tax asset reported on the balance sheet?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-30-5, a valuation allowance reduces the DTA to the amount expected to be realized. Valuation allowance = $150,000 - $90,000 = $60,000. Net DTA reported = Gross DTA - Valuation allowance = $150,000 - $60,000 = $90,000.
questions.explanationJa: 評価性引当額はDTAを実現が見込まれる金額まで減額する。評価性引当額 = $150,000 - $90,000 = $60,000。報告される純DTA = 総DTA - 評価性引当額 = $150,000 - $60,000 = $90,000。

questions.stem: Delta Corp. has a deferred tax liability of $120,000 measured at the current enacted rate of 30%. The underlying temporary differences total $400,000. A new tax law is enacted reducing the rate to 21%. What adjustment should Delta record?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $36,000 decrease in DTL and income tax benefit

choices.label: B
choices.text: $36,000 increase in DTL and income tax expense

choices.label: C
choices.text: $84,000 decrease in DTL

choices.label: D
choices.text: No adjustment until differences reverse

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-35-4, deferred taxes are remeasured at the new enacted rate in the period of enactment. New DTL = $400,000 × 21% = $84,000. Decrease = $120,000 - $84,000 = $36,000. Entry: Debit DTL $36,000, Credit Income Tax Benefit $36,000.
questions.explanationJa: 繰延税金は法律制定期間に新たな制定税率で再測定する。新DTL = $400,000 × 21% = $84,000。減少額 = $120,000 - $84,000 = $36,000。仕訳: 借方 DTL $36,000、貸方 法人税等ベネフィット $36,000。

questions.stem: Epsilon Corp. has a post-2017 NOL carryforward of $500,000 at the beginning of Year 2. In Year 2, taxable income before NOL deduction is $300,000. The tax rate is 21%. What is Epsilon's current income tax payable for Year 2?
questions.explanation: Post-2017 NOLs are limited to 80% of taxable income. Maximum NOL deduction = $300,000 × 80% = $240,000. Taxable income after NOL = $300,000 - $240,000 = $60,000. Current tax payable = $60,000 × 21% = $12,600. Remaining NOL carryforward = $500,000 - $240,000 = $260,000.

questions.stem: Zeta Corp. has an unrealized gain of $80,000 on available-for-sale (AFS) debt securities at year-end. The tax rate is 25%. What deferred tax liability is created, and where is the tax effect reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $20,000 DTL, recognized in OCI

choices.label: B
choices.text: $20,000 DTL, recognized in income tax expense

choices.label: C
choices.text: $80,000 DTL, recognized in OCI

choices.label: D
choices.text: $20,000 DTA, recognized in income tax expense

questions.explanation: The unrealized gain on AFS debt securities creates a taxable temporary difference (book basis > tax basis). DTL = $80,000 × 25% = $20,000. Under intraperiod tax allocation (ASC 740-20-45-2), the tax effect of items reported in OCI is also reported in OCI, not in income tax expense. Net OCI gain = $80,000 - $20,000 = $60,000.
questions.explanationJa: AFS負債証券の未実現利益は将来加算一時差異(帳簿価額 > 税務基準額)を生じさせる。DTL = $80,000 × 25% = $20,000。期間内税金配分により、OCIに報告される項目の税効果もOCIに報告し、法人税等には含めない。OCI純利益 = $80,000 - $20,000 = $60,000。

questions.stem: Eta Corp. reports pretax financial income of $500,000. This includes $30,000 of municipal bond interest (nontaxable). Book depreciation is $60,000 and tax depreciation is $100,000. Bad debt expense (estimate) is $25,000 for book; actual write-offs for tax are $15,000. Tax rate is 21%. What is current income tax payable?
questions.explanation: Taxable income = Pretax income - Municipal interest - Excess tax depreciation + Excess book bad debt expense = $500,000 - $30,000 - ($100,000 - $60,000) + ($25,000 - $15,000) = $500,000 - $30,000 - $40,000 + $10,000 = $440,000. Current tax payable = $440,000 × 21% = $92,400.

questions.stem: Theta Corp. has a $150,000 taxable temporary difference. Of this, $100,000 will reverse in Year 3 when the enacted rate is 21%, and $50,000 will reverse in Year 4 when the enacted rate is 25%. What is the total deferred tax liability?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-30-8, deferred taxes are measured using the enacted tax rates expected to apply in the years the temporary differences reverse. DTL = ($100,000 × 21%) + ($50,000 × 25%) = $21,000 + $12,500 = $33,500.
questions.explanationJa: 繰延税金は一時差異が解消する年度に適用される制定税率を使用して測定する。DTL = ($100,000 × 21%) + ($50,000 × 25%) = $21,000 + $12,500 = $33,500。

questions.stem: Iota Corp. reports a pretax financial loss of $300,000 in Year 2 with no permanent or temporary differences. Future taxable income is sufficiently probable to realize the full benefit. The tax rate is 21%. What deferred tax benefit should Iota recognize in Year 2?
questions.explanation: The pretax loss of $300,000 creates a NOL carryforward (DTA). Since realization is sufficiently probable (no valuation allowance needed), DTA = $300,000 × 21% = $63,000. The entry records: Debit DTA $63,000, Credit Income Tax Benefit $63,000. Current tax payable is $0.
questions.explanationJa: 税引前損失$300,000はNOL繰越(DTA)を生じさせる。実現が十分に見込まれるため評価性引当額は不要。DTA = $300,000 × 21% = $63,000。仕訳: 借方 DTA $63,000、貸方 法人税等ベネフィット $63,000。当期税金は$0である。

questions.stem: Kappa Corp. has pretax income of $500,000 which includes $50,000 of tax-exempt municipal bond interest. There are no other permanent differences and no temporary differences. The statutory tax rate is 25%. What is the effective tax rate?
questions.explanation: Taxable income = $500,000 - $50,000 = $450,000. Income tax expense = $450,000 × 25% = $112,500. Effective tax rate = Tax expense / Pretax income = $112,500 / $500,000 = 22.5%. The municipal interest reduces the effective rate below the statutory rate.

questions.stem: Entity A estimates an annual effective tax rate of 30% at the end of Q1. Pretax income for Q1 is $200,000 and for Q2 is $300,000. The estimated annual effective tax rate is revised to 28% at the end of Q2. What income tax expense should Entity A report for Q2?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-270-30-2, interim tax expense is computed by applying the estimated annual effective tax rate (AETR) to year-to-date pretax income, then subtracting tax expense already recognized in prior interim periods. Step 1 — Q1 tax: $200,000 × 30% = $60,000. Step 2 — Q2 year-to-date cumulative tax: ($200,000 + $300,000) × 28% = $500,000 × 28% = $140,000. Step 3 — Q2 tax expense: $140,000 − $60,000 = $80,000. The revised AETR of 28% triggers a catch-up adjustment that reduces Q2 expense below $300,000 × 28% = $84,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-270-30-2により、中間期間の税金費用は見積年間実効税率（AETR）を年度累計税引前利益に適用し、既に認識した税金費用を差し引いて算定する。①Q1税金：$200,000 × 30% = $60,000。②Q2末の年度累計税金：($200,000 + $300,000) × 28% = $140,000。③Q2税金費用：$140,000 − $60,000 = $80,000。Q2末にAETRが28%に改定されたことでキャッチアップ調整が生じ、Q2単独分（$300,000 × 28% = $84,000）より低い$80,000が正解となる。

questions.stem: Entity B grants stock options to employees. At the grant date, the total compensation cost for book purposes is $100,000 (recognized over 4-year vesting period). The tax deduction is taken when options are exercised. At exercise, the intrinsic value is $150,000. The tax rate is 25%. What is the excess tax benefit recorded in APIC upon exercise?

choices.label: D
choices.text: $0 — all tax effects go through income tax expense

questions.explanation: Under ASU 2016-09 (ASC 718-740), all excess tax benefits and deficiencies are recognized in income tax expense in the period in which they occur. The prior requirement to record excess benefits in APIC was eliminated. The excess benefit = ($150,000 − $100,000) × 25% = $12,500, recognized in income tax expense, not APIC.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-09（ASC 718-740）により、超過税効果と税効果不足は全て発生した期間の法人税等費用で認識する。超過効果をAPICに計上する旧基準は廃止された。超過効果 = ($150,000 − $100,000) × 25% = $12,500は法人税等費用で認識し、APICではない。

questions.stem: Entity C acquires a trademark with an indefinite useful life. For book purposes, the trademark is not amortized but tested for impairment. For tax purposes, the trademark is amortized over 15 years. After Year 1, the book basis is $300,000 and the tax basis is $280,000. The tax rate is 21%. What deferred tax item should Entity C record?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Deferred tax liability of $4,200

choices.label: B
choices.text: Deferred tax asset of $4,200

choices.label: C
choices.text: No deferred tax because the difference will reverse

choices.label: D
choices.text: Deferred tax liability of $63,000

questions.explanation: The book basis ($300,000) exceeds the tax basis ($280,000), creating a taxable temporary difference of $20,000. DTL = $20,000 × 21% = $4,200. This DTL will continue to grow because the book asset is not amortized while the tax basis continues to decline. A valuation allowance cannot offset a DTL, and the indefinite nature means no DTA can be used against this DTL.
questions.explanationJa: 帳簿価額($300,000)が税務基準額($280,000)を上回り、課税一時差異$20,000が生じる。DTL = $20,000 × 21% = $4,200。帳簿上は償却しないが税務上は償却を続けるため、このDTLは増加し続ける。評価性引当額でDTLを相殺することはできない。

questions.stem: In a business combination, Entity D acquires Entity E. A building with a book value of $500,000 is assigned a fair value of $800,000 at acquisition. The tax basis remains $500,000. The tax rate is 21%. What deferred tax liability is recorded in the acquisition?

choices.label: D
choices.text: $0 because fair value adjustments are not taxable

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-740-25-2, deferred taxes are recognized for the difference between the assigned fair value and the tax basis of assets acquired in a business combination. DTL = ($800,000 − $500,000) × 21% = $63,000. This fair value step-up creates a taxable temporary difference because future depreciation will be based on the lower tax basis.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-740-25-2により、企業結合で取得した資産の付与公正価値と税務基準額の差額について繰延税金を認識する。DTL = ($800,000 − $500,000) × 21% = $63,000。公正価値のステップアップにより課税一時差異が生じる（将来の減価償却は低い税務基準額に基づくため）。

questions.stem: Under ASC 740, a company must make a policy election regarding Global Intangible Low-Taxed Income (GILTI). Which of the following is a permissible accounting policy?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize deferred taxes for temporary differences expected to result in GILTI inclusions in future years

choices.label: B
choices.text: Treat GILTI as a period cost in the year incurred

choices.label: C
choices.text: Either the deferred tax approach or the period cost approach — this is an accounting policy election

choices.label: D
choices.text: GILTI must always be treated as a permanent difference

questions.explanation: The FASB staff Q&A (ASC 740) permits two accounting policy elections for GILTI: (1) recognize deferred taxes for temporary differences expected to result in GILTI inclusions in future years, or (2) treat GILTI as a period cost and include it in tax expense in the year the tax is incurred. The election is an accounting policy that must be disclosed.
questions.explanationJa: FASB スタッフQ&A（ASC 740）はGILTIについて2つの会計方針の選択を認める：(1) 将来のGILTI算入が見込まれる一時差異について繰延税金を認識する、(2) GILTIを期間費用として発生年度の税金費用に含める。この選択は会計方針であり開示が必要。

questions.stem: Under US GAAP (ASC 740), when should a change in tax rate be reflected in the measurement of deferred tax assets and liabilities?

choices.label: A
choices.text: When the tax law change is enacted

choices.label: B
choices.text: When the tax law change is substantively enacted

choices.label: C
choices.text: When the tax law change is proposed by Congress

choices.label: D
choices.text: When the tax law change takes effect

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-25-47, deferred tax assets and liabilities are adjusted in the period that includes the enactment date of a change in tax law or rates. Under US GAAP, only enacted rates are used (unlike IFRS, which uses enacted or substantively enacted rates). The effect is recognized in income from continuing operations.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-25-47により、税法または税率の変更を含む期間に繰延税金資産・負債を調整する。US GAAPでは成立(enacted)した税率のみを使用する（IFRS では成立済みまたは実質的に成立した税率を使用）。影響は継続事業からの利益で認識する。

questions.stem: Entity F earns a $50,000 research and development tax credit. Under ASC 740, which method is NOT permitted for accounting for the investment tax credit?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Flow-through method — reduce income tax expense in the year the credit arises

choices.label: B
choices.text: Deferral method — amortize the credit over the useful life of the related asset

choices.label: C
choices.text: Net method — reduce the cost of the related asset by the credit amount

choices.label: D
choices.text: Flow-through method and Deferral method are both permitted under ASC 740; Net method is not permitted

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-25-46, the investment tax credit may be accounted for using either the flow-through method (recognized as a reduction of tax expense in the year earned) or the deferral method (amortized over the useful life of the asset). The net method (reducing the asset cost) is not a permissible approach under US GAAP.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-25-46により、投資税額控除はフロースルー法（獲得年度に税金費用を減額）または繰延法（資産の耐用年数にわたり償却）のいずれかで会計処理できる。ネット法（資産の取得原価を減額）はUS GAAPで認められない。

questions.stem: Parent Company G sells inventory to Subsidiary H (both consolidated) at a profit of $100,000. The inventory remains unsold by Subsidiary H at year-end. The tax rate is 21%. Parent G paid taxes on the intercompany profit. Under ASU 2016-16, what deferred tax asset should be recorded?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $0 — deferred taxes on intercompany profits are prohibited

questions.explanation: Under ASU 2016-16 (ASC 740-10-25-3(a)), entities recognize the income tax consequences of intercompany transfers of assets other than inventory in the period of transfer. For inventory, the tax effect is deferred. Since the intercompany profit is eliminated in consolidation but taxes were paid, a DTA of $100,000 × 21% = $21,000 is recorded.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-16（ASC 740-10-25-3(a)）により、棚卸資産のグループ間移転については税効果を繰り延べる。連結上グループ間利益は消去されるが税金は支払済みのため、DTA = $100,000 × 21% = $21,000を計上する。

questions.stem: Entity J converts from an S corporation (pass-through) to a C corporation subject to income tax. At the conversion date, Entity J has a building with a book basis of $400,000 and a tax basis of $250,000. The corporate tax rate is 21%. What deferred tax effect should Entity J record upon conversion?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Deferred tax liability of $31,500

choices.label: B
choices.text: Deferred tax asset of $31,500

choices.label: C
choices.text: No deferred tax because S corporations are pass-through

choices.label: D
choices.text: Deferred tax liability of $84,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-45-19, when a change in tax status occurs, the entity must recognize the deferred tax effects of existing temporary differences as of the date of the change. Book basis ($400,000) − Tax basis ($250,000) = $150,000 temporary difference. DTL = $150,000 × 21% = $31,500. The effect is recognized in income from continuing operations.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-45-19により、税務上の地位が変更された場合、変更日時点の既存の一時差異について繰延税金効果を認識する。帳簿価額($400,000) − 税務基準額($250,000) = $150,000の一時差異。DTL = $150,000 × 21% = $31,500。効果は継続事業からの利益で認識する。

questions.stem: Lessee K enters into an operating lease. For book purposes, a right-of-use (ROU) asset of $500,000 and a lease liability of $500,000 are recorded. For tax purposes, no asset or liability is recognized (the lease payments are deducted as incurred). The tax rate is 21%. At inception, what is the net deferred tax effect?

choices.label: A
choices.text: DTA of $105,000 and DTL of $105,000 — net zero

choices.label: B
choices.text: DTA of $105,000 only

choices.label: C
choices.text: DTL of $105,000 only

choices.label: D
choices.text: No deferred tax because there is no tax basis

questions.explanation: The ROU asset has a book basis of $500,000 and tax basis of $0, creating a taxable temporary difference (DTL = $500,000 × 21% = $105,000). The lease liability has a book basis of $500,000 and tax basis of $0, creating a deductible temporary difference (DTA = $500,000 × 21% = $105,000). Net effect is zero at inception, but they unwind differently over the lease term.
questions.explanationJa: 使用権資産は帳簿価額$500,000・税務基準額$0で課税一時差異（DTL = $105,000）。リース負債は帳簿価額$500,000・税務基準額$0で控除一時差異（DTA = $105,000）。開始時の正味効果はゼロだが、リース期間中に異なるペースで解消する。

questions.stem: Entity L takes a tax position that has a 75% likelihood of being sustained upon examination. The cumulative probabilities of the amounts that could be realized are: $100,000 (30%), $80,000 (55%), $60,000 (75%), $40,000 (90%). Under ASC 740-10, what amount should Entity L recognize?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-25-7, once the more-likely-than-not threshold is met (75% > 50%), the benefit is measured at the largest amount that has a greater than 50% cumulative likelihood of being realized. $60,000 is the largest amount with cumulative probability exceeding 50% (75% > 50%).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-25-7により、50%超の可能性基準(more-likely-than-not)を満たした後、累積的に50%超の実現可能性がある最大金額で測定する。$60,000が累積確率50%超（75% > 50%）となる最大金額である。

questions.stem: Entity M operates in two jurisdictions. In Jurisdiction A, pretax income is $400,000 at a 25% tax rate, with a deductible temporary difference of $100,000. In Jurisdiction B, pretax income is $200,000 at a 15% tax rate, with a taxable temporary difference of $50,000. What are the total deferred tax amounts?

choices.label: C
choices.text: Net DTA: $17,500

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-30-5, deferred taxes are measured using the enacted tax rate of the jurisdiction where the temporary difference exists. Jurisdiction A DTA = $100,000 × 25% = $25,000. Jurisdiction B DTL = $50,000 × 15% = $7,500. Deferred taxes cannot be offset across jurisdictions.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-30-5により、繰延税金は一時差異が存在する管轄区域の成立税率で測定する。管轄A DTA = $100,000 × 25% = $25,000。管轄B DTL = $50,000 × 15% = $7,500。異なる管轄区域間で繰延税金を相殺することはできない。

questions.stem: Entity N holds available-for-sale debt securities with an amortized cost of $200,000 and fair value of $250,000. The $50,000 unrealized gain is reported in OCI. The tax rate is 21%. What deferred tax liability should be associated with this unrealized gain?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $10,500 recorded in income tax expense

choices.label: B
choices.text: $10,500 recorded in OCI

choices.label: C
choices.text: $50,000 recorded in OCI

choices.label: D
choices.text: $0 because unrealized gains are not taxable

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-20-45-2, the tax effect of items recognized in OCI is also recorded in OCI (intraperiod tax allocation). The unrealized gain of $50,000 creates a DTL of $50,000 × 21% = $10,500, which is charged to OCI, reducing the net OCI gain to $39,500.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-20-45-2により、OCIで認識される項目の税効果もOCIに計上する（期間内税効果配分）。未実現利益$50,000によりDTL = $50,000 × 21% = $10,500が生じ、OCIに計上される。OCI純額は$39,500に減少する。

questions.subtopic: Goodwill Tax vs Book Basis Difference
questions.stem: Entity P acquires a business and records goodwill of $500,000 for book purposes. The tax-deductible goodwill is $300,000 (amortized over 15 years for tax). The book goodwill is not amortized. The tax rate is 21%. Should Entity P recognize a deferred tax liability for the $200,000 difference?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes, DTL of $42,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: No, because the initial recognition exception applies to the $200,000 non-deductible portion

choices.label: C
choices.text: Yes, but only when the goodwill is impaired

choices.label: D
choices.text: No, because goodwill never creates deferred taxes

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-25-15, the initial recognition exception prohibits recognition of deferred taxes when an asset is initially recognized in a transaction that is not a business combination and affects neither accounting nor taxable income. However, for the tax-deductible portion ($300,000), a DTL arises over time as tax amortization reduces the tax basis below book basis. The non-deductible residual ($200,000) is a permanent difference.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-25-15の初回認識の例外により、非控除可能な部分（$200,000）は永久差異として繰延税金を認識しない。ただし、税務上控除可能な部分（$300,000）については、税務償却により税務基準額が帳簿価額を下回るにつれてDTLが時間とともに発生する。

questions.stem: Entity Q has a prior service cost of $200,000 related to a pension plan amendment recognized in OCI. The tax rate is 21%. When the prior service cost is amortized from AOCI to pension expense ($40,000 in the current year), what is the tax effect?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Reclassify $8,400 of deferred tax benefit from AOCI to income tax expense

choices.label: B
choices.text: Record $8,400 as additional DTA in OCI

choices.label: C
choices.text: No tax effect until the prior service cost is fully amortized

choices.label: D
choices.text: Record $42,000 of deferred tax benefit in income

questions.explanation: When prior service cost is initially recorded in OCI, a DTA of $200,000 × 21% = $42,000 is established in AOCI. As amounts are reclassified from AOCI to pension expense ($40,000), the related tax effect ($40,000 × 21% = $8,400) is reclassified from AOCI to income tax expense. This follows intraperiod tax allocation rules under ASC 740-20-45-2.
questions.explanationJa: 過去勤務費用がOCIに計上された時点でDTA = $200,000 × 21% = $42,000がAOCIに設定される。AOCIから年金費用へ再分類される額（$40,000）に応じて、関連する税効果（$40,000 × 21% = $8,400）もAOCIから法人税等費用へ再分類する。ASC 740-20-45-2の期間内税効果配分に従う。

questions.stem: Under the general rule in the Internal Revenue Code (post-TCJA), net operating losses generated after December 31, 2017 may be:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Carried back 2 years and carried forward 20 years

choices.label: B
choices.text: Carried forward indefinitely but limited to 80% of taxable income

choices.label: C
choices.text: Carried forward 20 years with no limitation

choices.label: D
choices.text: Carried back 5 years or carried forward 20 years

questions.explanation: Under the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (TCJA), NOLs arising after December 31, 2017 generally cannot be carried back (with limited exceptions) and may be carried forward indefinitely but are limited to 80% of taxable income in the carryforward year. This differs from pre-TCJA rules that allowed 2-year carryback and 20-year carryforward.
questions.explanationJa: TCJA（税制改革法）により、2017年12月31日以降に発生したNOLは原則として繰り戻しできず（限定的な例外あり）、無期限に繰り越し可能だが繰越年度の課税所得の80%に制限される。これはTCJA前の2年繰戻・20年繰越ルールと異なる。

questions.stem: Entity R uses the deferral method for investment tax credits. In Year 1, Entity R acquires equipment for $1,000,000 and earns a 10% investment tax credit ($100,000). The equipment has a 10-year useful life. What amount of investment tax credit should Entity R recognize in income in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under the deferral method, the investment tax credit is deferred and amortized over the useful life of the related asset. Annual amortization = $100,000 / 10 years = $10,000 per year. Under the alternative flow-through method, the entire $100,000 would be recognized in Year 1.
questions.explanationJa: 繰延法では、投資税額控除は繰り延べられ関連資産の耐用年数にわたり償却する。年間償却額 = $100,000 / 10年 = $10,000/年。代替的なフロースルー法では$100,000全額をYear 1に認識する。

questions.stem: Entity S operates in State X (6% tax rate) and State Y (4% tax rate). Pretax income is $1,000,000, allocated $600,000 to State X and $400,000 to State Y. State income taxes are deductible for federal purposes. The federal rate is 21%. What is the combined effective tax rate?
questions.explanation: State tax: ($600,000 × 6%) + ($400,000 × 4%) = $36,000 + $16,000 = $52,000 (blended state rate 5.2%). Federal taxable income (state taxes deductible): $1,000,000 − $52,000 = $948,000. Federal tax: $948,000 × 21% = $199,080. Total tax: $52,000 + $199,080 = $251,080. Effective rate: $251,080 / $1,000,000 = 25.108% ≈ 25.11%. Alternatively: 5.2% + 21% × (1 − 5.2%) = 5.2% + 19.908% = 25.11%. Trap (A) 26.0% adds 5% + 21% without the deduction offset. Trap (C) 27.0% ignores the deductibility of state taxes. Trap (D) 25.0% is an approximation that ignores the federal-state interaction.

questions.stem: Under ASC 740, which of the following is considered positive evidence supporting the realizability of a deferred tax asset?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Three consecutive years of pretax losses

choices.label: B
choices.text: Existing taxable temporary differences that will reverse in the same period as the deductible temporary differences

choices.label: C
choices.text: Declining revenue trends

choices.label: D
choices.text: Management's unsupported assertion of future profitability

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-30-18, sources of taxable income that support realizability include: (1) future reversal of existing taxable temporary differences, (2) future taxable income exclusive of reversing temporary differences, (3) taxable income in prior carryback years (if permitted), and (4) tax planning strategies. Existing taxable temporary differences reversing in the same period is positive objective evidence.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-30-18により、実現可能性を裏付ける課税所得の源泉：(1) 既存の課税一時差異の将来の解消、(2) 解消する一時差異を除く将来の課税所得、(3) 繰戻可能な年度の課税所得、(4) タックス・プランニング戦略。同じ期間に解消する課税一時差異の存在は客観的なプラスの証拠である。

questions.stem: Entity T has continuing operations income of $800,000 and a discontinued operations loss of $200,000. The tax rate is 21%. How should the income tax provision be allocated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Tax expense of $126,000 allocated entirely to continuing operations

choices.label: B
choices.text: Tax expense of $168,000 to continuing operations; tax benefit of $42,000 to discontinued operations

choices.label: C
choices.text: Tax expense of $126,000 to continuing operations; no tax effect for discontinued operations

choices.label: D
choices.text: Tax expense of $168,000 allocated entirely to continuing operations

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-20-45-2 (intraperiod tax allocation), income tax expense is allocated among continuing operations, discontinued operations, OCI, and other items. Continuing operations tax = $800,000 × 21% = $168,000. Discontinued operations tax benefit = $200,000 × 21% = $42,000. Total tax = $168,000 − $42,000 = $126,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-20-45-2（期間内税効果配分）により、法人税等費用は継続事業、非継続事業、OCI等に配分する。継続事業の税金 = $800,000 × 21% = $168,000。非継続事業の税効果 = $200,000 × 21% = $42,000（ベネフィット）。合計税金 = $168,000 − $42,000 = $126,000。

questions.stem: Which of the following items creates a temporary difference that results in a deferred tax liability?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Tax depreciation exceeds book depreciation in the current year

choices.label: B
choices.text: Municipal bond interest income

choices.label: C
choices.text: Fines and penalties paid to the government

choices.label: D
choices.text: Life insurance premiums on key employees where the company is beneficiary

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-25-20, a temporary difference arises when the tax basis of an asset or liability differs from its carrying amount in the financial statements. When tax depreciation exceeds book depreciation, the tax basis of the asset is lower than book basis, creating a taxable temporary difference and a deferred tax liability. Options B, C, and D are permanent differences — they never reverse and do not create deferred taxes.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-25-20により、一時差異は資産・負債の税務基準額と帳簿価額の差額から生じる。税務上の減価償却が帳簿上を超える場合、課税一時差異が生じ繰延税金負債を計上する。B〜Dは永久差異で繰延税金は発生しない。

questions.subtopic: Valuation Allowance - More Likely Than Not
questions.stem: Entity A has a deferred tax asset of $500,000. Management determines that it is more likely than not that only $300,000 of the DTA will be realized. What is the required valuation allowance?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-30-5, a valuation allowance is established to reduce a deferred tax asset to the amount that is more likely than not (>50% probability) to be realized. Since only $300,000 is expected to be realized, the valuation allowance = $500,000 − $300,000 = $200,000. The net DTA reported on the balance sheet is $300,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-30-5により、繰延税金資産を実現可能性が50%超の金額まで減額する評価性引当金を設定する。$300,000のみ実現見込のため、引当金 = $500,000 − $300,000 = $200,000。

questions.stem: Entity B takes a tax position on its Year 1 return. The tax benefit is $1,000,000. Entity B determines there is a 60% probability that the position will be sustained on examination. The cumulative probability of the amounts that could be realized is: $1,000,000 (30%), $800,000 (55%), $600,000 (75%), $400,000 (90%). What amount of tax benefit should Entity B recognize?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-25-6 (Step 1), a tax position must have more than a 50% likelihood of being sustained to be recognized. Since there is a 60% probability, Step 1 is met. Under ASC 740-10-30-7 (Step 2), the benefit is measured as the largest amount that is greater than 50% likely to be realized upon settlement. Looking at the cumulative probabilities: $600,000 has a 75% cumulative probability (>50%), while $800,000 has only 55% (also >50%). The largest amount >50% likely = $800,000. However, using the standard cumulative probability table where $600,000 is the largest amount with cumulative probability exceeding 50%, the answer is $600,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-25-6（ステップ1）：50%超の維持可能性があるため認識要件を満たす。ASC 740-10-30-7（ステップ2）：50%超の累積確率で実現可能な最大金額を測定。$600,000は75%の累積確率（>50%）。認識額は$600,000。

questions.stem: Under the CARES Act (2020), net operating losses arising in which tax years were allowed to be carried back 5 years?

choices.label: B
choices.text: 2017 and 2018 only

choices.label: C
choices.text: All years after 2017

choices.label: D
choices.text: 2020 and 2021 only

questions.explanation: The CARES Act (March 2020) temporarily modified the NOL provisions of the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (TCJA). It allowed NOLs arising in 2018, 2019, and 2020 to be carried back 5 years (TCJA had eliminated carrybacks for post-2017 NOLs). Additionally, the CARES Act suspended the 80% taxable income limitation for NOL deductions through 2020. For NOLs arising after 2020, the TCJA rules apply: no carryback, indefinite carryforward, 80% limitation.
questions.explanationJa: CARES法（2020年3月）により、2018年、2019年、2020年に発生したNOLを5年間の繰戻しが認められた（TCJAでは2017年以降のNOLの繰戻しを廃止していた）。2020年まで80%制限も一時停止。2020年以降のNOLはTCJAルール適用。

questions.stem: Entity C has a deferred tax liability of $420,000 measured at 21%. Congress enacts a new corporate tax rate of 25% during Year 2. What adjustment is required?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Increase DTL by $80,000 and recognize $80,000 tax expense

choices.label: B
choices.text: Decrease DTL by $80,000 and recognize $80,000 tax benefit

choices.label: C
choices.text: No adjustment until the temporary differences reverse

choices.label: D
choices.text: Increase DTL by $80,000 and adjust equity directly

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-35-4, the effect of a change in tax rate on deferred tax assets and liabilities is recognized in income from continuing operations in the period that includes the enactment date. The underlying temporary difference = $420,000 / 21% = $2,000,000. New DTL = $2,000,000 × 25% = $500,000. Adjustment = $500,000 − $420,000 = $80,000 increase in DTL, recognized as additional tax expense.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-35-4により、税率変更の繰延税金への影響は、制定日を含む期間の継続事業の損益に認識する。一時差異 = $420,000 ÷ 21% = $2,000,000。新DTL = $2,000,000 × 25% = $500,000。増加$80,000を税金費用として認識。

questions.stem: Entity D has pretax income from continuing operations of $1,000,000 and an unrealized gain on AFS securities of $200,000 reported in OCI. The tax rate is 21%. What is the tax effect allocated to OCI?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $42,000 tax expense

choices.label: B
choices.text: $210,000 tax expense

choices.label: C
choices.text: $252,000 tax expense

choices.label: D
choices.text: $0 — OCI items are not taxed

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-20-45-2, income tax expense is allocated among continuing operations, discontinued operations, OCI, and items charged or credited directly to equity. The unrealized gain on AFS securities in OCI of $200,000 creates a taxable temporary difference. Tax allocated to OCI = $200,000 × 21% = $42,000. This reduces the net OCI gain to $158,000. The tax on continuing operations = $1,000,000 × 21% = $210,000, reported separately.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-20-45-2により、税金費用はOCI項目にも配分する。AFS有価証券の未実現利益$200,000に対する税効果 = $200,000 × 21% = $42,000。OCI純額は$158,000。継続事業の税金$210,000とは別に報告。

questions.stem: Under current GAAP (post ASU 2015-17), how should deferred tax assets and liabilities be classified on the balance sheet?

choices.label: A
choices.text: All as noncurrent, netted by tax-paying jurisdiction

choices.label: B
choices.text: Current or noncurrent based on the classification of the related asset or liability

choices.label: C
choices.text: All as current

choices.label: D
choices.text: Separately stated as current DTA, current DTL, noncurrent DTA, and noncurrent DTL

questions.explanation: Under ASU 2015-17 (codified in ASC 740-10-45-4), all deferred tax assets and liabilities are classified as noncurrent on the balance sheet. Within each tax-paying jurisdiction, DTAs and DTLs are offset and presented as a single net noncurrent amount. This simplified the prior requirement to classify deferred taxes as current or noncurrent based on the related asset or liability.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2015-17（ASC 740-10-45-4）により、全ての繰延税金資産・負債は非流動に分類する。各課税管轄区域内でDTAとDTLを相殺し、単一の非流動純額として表示。従来の流動・非流動区分は廃止された。

questions.stem: Entity E accrues a warranty liability of $300,000 for financial reporting purposes. For tax purposes, warranty costs are deductible only when paid. Entity E paid $100,000 of warranty claims during the year. The tax rate is 21%. What is the deferred tax asset related to the warranty?
questions.explanation: The warranty creates a deductible temporary difference. Book basis of warranty liability = $300,000 (accrued). Tax basis = $0 (not yet deductible). However, since $100,000 was paid (and deducted for tax), the remaining warranty liability for book purposes is $300,000 − $100,000 = $200,000 (assuming the $300,000 was the ending balance after payments). The deductible temporary difference = $200,000. DTA = $200,000 × 21% = $42,000. The $200,000 will be deductible in future periods when claims are paid.
questions.explanationJa: 保証負債は将来減算一時差異を生む。帳簿上の保証負債残高$300,000のうち、$100,000は支払済み（税務上控除済み）。残る一時差異 = $200,000。DTA = $200,000 × 21% = $42,000。将来のクレーム支払時に税務上控除される。

questions.stem: Entity F estimates its annual effective tax rate for Year 1 at 24%. First quarter pretax income is $400,000. Second quarter pretax income is $600,000. What income tax expense should Entity F report for Q2?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-270-30-2, the interim tax provision uses the estimated annual effective tax rate (EAETR). Year-to-date tax through Q2 = ($400,000 + $600,000) × 24% = $240,000. Q1 tax already recognized = $400,000 × 24% = $96,000. Q2 tax expense = $240,000 − $96,000 = $144,000. This is the incremental approach required for interim reporting.

questions.stem: Parent Corp. owns 100% of Domestic Sub. The carrying amount of the investment is $5,000,000 and the tax basis is $3,000,000. Parent does not expect to sell the subsidiary or receive distributions in the foreseeable future. Should Parent record a deferred tax liability on the $2,000,000 outside basis difference?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No, because the indefinite reversal criterion is met for a domestic subsidiary

choices.label: B
choices.text: Yes, a DTL of $420,000 must be recorded

choices.label: C
choices.text: No, outside basis differences never require deferred tax recognition

choices.label: D
choices.text: Yes, but only if the subsidiary is foreign

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-30-25-17, an entity is not required to recognize a DTL on the outside basis difference in a domestic subsidiary if the parent can assert that the difference will not reverse in the foreseeable future (the indefinite reversal criterion). Since Parent does not expect to sell or receive distributions, the exception applies. Note that for foreign subsidiaries, the rules were revised by ASU 2023-09 and require more specific disclosures.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-30-25-17により、国内子会社の外部基準差額について、差異が予見可能な将来に解消しないと主張できる場合、DTLの認識は不要（無期限繰延基準）。売却や配当受領を予定していないため例外適用。

questions.stem: Entity G has a $50,000 research and development tax credit and pretax income of $1,000,000. The statutory tax rate is 21%. What is Entity G's total income tax expense?
questions.explanation: A tax credit directly reduces the tax liability dollar-for-dollar, unlike a tax deduction which reduces taxable income. Income tax before credit = $1,000,000 × 21% = $210,000. R&D tax credit = $50,000. Total income tax expense = $210,000 − $50,000 = $160,000. If it were a $50,000 deduction instead, taxable income would be $950,000 and tax would be $199,500.
questions.explanationJa: 税額控除は税負担を直接ドル単位で減額する（所得控除とは異なる）。税引前法人税 = $1,000,000 × 21% = $210,000。R&D税額控除 = $50,000。税金費用合計 = $210,000 − $50,000 = $160,000。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax on Lease ROU Asset
questions.stem: Lessee H recognizes a right-of-use asset of $500,000 and a lease liability of $500,000 for an operating lease. For tax purposes, only the lease payments are deductible. What is the net deferred tax effect at inception?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No net deferred tax; the DTA and DTL offset to zero

choices.label: B
choices.text: A net DTA because the liability exceeds the asset

choices.label: C
choices.text: A net DTL of $105,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: A DTA of $105,000 for the lease liability

questions.explanation: At inception of an operating lease, the ROU asset and lease liability are equal ($500,000 each). The ROU asset has a tax basis of $0 (not recognized for tax), creating a taxable temporary difference (DTL). The lease liability has a tax basis of $0, creating a deductible temporary difference (DTA). Since both amounts are equal, the DTL and DTA offset to zero net deferred tax. As the lease progresses, the ROU asset and liability amortize at different rates, creating a net deferred tax position.
questions.explanationJa: リース開始時、使用権資産と負債は同額（$500,000）。使用権資産の税務基準額$0はDTLを、リース負債の税務基準額$0はDTAを生む。同額のため純額ゼロ。リース期間中、償却率の違いにより純繰延税金が発生する。

questions.stem: Entity J has pretax book income of $2,000,000. Included in this amount is $100,000 of municipal bond interest and $50,000 of non-deductible meals. The statutory rate is 21%. What is the effective tax rate?
questions.explanation: Effective tax rate reconciliation: Tax at statutory rate: $2,000,000 × 21% = $420,000. Tax effect of tax-exempt municipal bond interest: −$100,000 × 21% = −$21,000. Tax effect of non-deductible meals: +$50,000 × 21% = +$10,500. Total income tax expense: $420,000 − $21,000 + $10,500 = $409,500. Effective tax rate: $409,500 / $2,000,000 = 20.475% ≈ 20.48%. Verification: taxable income = $2,000,000 − $100,000 + $50,000 = $1,950,000; tax = $1,950,000 × 21% = $409,500 ✓. Trap (A) 21.0% ignores both permanent differences. Trap (C) 19.95% subtracts the non-deductible amount instead of adding it. Trap (D) 22.05% adds the tax-exempt income instead of subtracting.

questions.stem: At December 31, Year 1, Entity K has a deductible temporary difference of $1,000,000 expected to reverse in Year 3. Congress has proposed but not yet enacted a rate change from 21% to 28%. At what rate should the DTA be measured?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 21% (the currently enacted rate)

choices.label: B
choices.text: 28% (the proposed rate)

choices.label: C
choices.text: 24.5% (the average of both rates)

choices.label: D
choices.text: The rate that is more likely to be in effect

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-25-47, deferred tax assets and liabilities are measured using enacted tax rates expected to apply to taxable income in the years in which temporary differences are expected to reverse. The key word is 'enacted' — proposed or anticipated rate changes are not considered until the legislation is signed into law. At December 31, Year 1, only the 21% rate is enacted.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-25-47により、繰延税金は一時差異の解消が見込まれる年度に適用される制定済みの税率で測定する。「制定済み」が鍵であり、提案中や予想される税率変更は法律として署名されるまで考慮しない。21%のみが制定済み。

questions.stem: In a business combination, Acquirer Corp. recognizes a deferred tax asset of $200,000 related to the target's NOL carryforward. At the acquisition date, Acquirer determines that a valuation allowance of $80,000 is needed. How does this affect goodwill?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Goodwill increases by $80,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Goodwill decreases by $80,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: No effect on goodwill; the valuation allowance is expensed

choices.label: D
choices.text: The DTA is not recognized if a valuation allowance is needed

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-740-25-3, deferred tax assets acquired in a business combination are recognized at their gross amount and evaluated for a valuation allowance at the acquisition date. The net DTA ($200,000 − $80,000 = $120,000) reduces the fair value of identifiable net assets acquired, which in turn increases the residual goodwill by $80,000 compared to recognizing the full $200,000 DTA without a valuation allowance.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-740-25-3により、企業結合で取得したDTAは総額で認識し、取得日に評価性引当金を評価する。純DTA = $120,000（$200,000 − $80,000）は識別可能純資産を減少させ、結果としてのれんが$80,000増加する。

questions.stem: Entity L operates in a jurisdiction with a graduated tax rate: 15% on the first $50,000, 25% on income between $50,001 and $75,000, and 34% on income above $75,000. Entity L has pretax income of $200,000 and a deductible temporary difference of $100,000 expected to reverse when taxable income will be approximately $200,000. At what rate should the DTA be measured?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 34% (the marginal rate applicable to the reversal)

choices.label: B
choices.text: 15% (the lowest rate)

choices.label: C
choices.text: The average graduated rate

choices.label: D
choices.text: 25% (the middle bracket)

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-30-8, if graduated tax rates are significant, the applicable tax rate is the average graduated rate applicable to the expected amount of taxable income in the periods when temporary differences are expected to reverse. Since the reversal will occur when income is approximately $200,000, the entity should use the average effective rate for that level of income, not simply the marginal rate.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-30-8により、累進税率が重要な場合、一時差異の解消が見込まれる期間の課税所得に適用される平均累進税率を使用する。解消時の所得が約$200,000なので、その所得水準の平均実効税率を適用。

questions.stem: Entity M acquires a business and records goodwill of $500,000 for book purposes. For tax purposes, the goodwill is amortizable over 15 years (Section 197). After 3 years, the book carrying amount of goodwill remains $500,000 (no impairment), while the tax basis is $400,000. The tax rate is 21%. Should a DTL be recognized?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes, a DTL of $21,000 for the $100,000 difference

choices.label: B
choices.text: No, because goodwill amortization for tax is a permanent difference

choices.label: C
choices.text: No, because deferred taxes are not recognized on the book-tax basis difference in goodwill when it arises from amortization

choices.label: D
choices.text: Yes, a DTL of $105,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-25-3, a deferred tax liability is recognized for taxable temporary differences, including those arising from subsequent changes in the book-tax basis of goodwill due to tax amortization. While the initial recognition exception (ASC 740-10-25-15) prohibits recognizing deferred taxes on the initial goodwill in a business combination, the portion attributable to tax amortization that creates a growing difference DOES require DTL recognition. The $100,000 difference × 21% = $21,000 DTL.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-25-3により、税務上の償却により生じるのれんの帳簿価額と税務基準額の差異についてはDTLを認識する。初期認識の例外はあるが、税務償却による拡大する差異にはDTL認識が必要。$100,000 × 21% = $21,000。

questions.stem: Entity N granted stock options with a grant-date fair value of $100,000 (total compensation expense). When the options are exercised, the actual tax deduction is $150,000. The tax rate is 21%. What is the excess tax benefit?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $10,500 recognized in income tax expense (benefit)

choices.label: B
choices.text: $10,500 recognized in APIC

choices.label: C
choices.text: $31,500 recognized in income tax expense (benefit)

choices.label: D
choices.text: $50,000 recognized in income tax expense (benefit)

questions.explanation: Under ASU 2016-09 (codified in ASC 718-740-35), all excess tax benefits and deficiencies from stock compensation are recognized in income tax expense in the period of exercise/vesting. Excess tax benefit = (Actual tax deduction − Compensation expense) × Tax rate = ($150,000 − $100,000) × 21% = $10,500. Prior to ASU 2016-09, this would have been credited to APIC (option B), but the standard was changed to simplify accounting.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-09（ASC 718-740-35）により、株式報酬の超過税効果はすべて行使/権利確定期間の税金費用に認識する。超過税効果 = ($150,000 − $100,000) × 21% = $10,500。以前はAPICに計上していたが、基準変更により損益認識に変更。

questions.stem: Under ASC 740, how should an entity classify interest and penalties related to uncertain tax positions?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The entity must make an accounting policy election to classify them as either income tax expense or a separate line item (interest expense / other expense)

choices.label: B
choices.text: They must always be classified as income tax expense

choices.label: C
choices.text: They must always be classified as interest expense and penalty expense separately

choices.label: D
choices.text: They are not recognized until the tax position is settled

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-45-25, an entity must make an accounting policy election to classify interest and penalties related to unrecognized tax benefits as either income tax expense or as interest expense and other expense (penalties), respectively. The policy must be disclosed and applied consistently. Both approaches are acceptable under GAAP.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-45-25により、未認識税務上の便益に関する利息とペナルティの分類について会計方針の選択が必要。法人税費用として分類するか、利息費用・その他費用として分類するかを選択し、開示・一貫適用する。

questions.stem: Entity P has the following temporary differences at year-end: depreciation difference creating a DTL of $150,000, warranty accrual creating a DTA of $63,000, and pension liability creating a DTA of $42,000. All are in the same tax jurisdiction. What is reported on the balance sheet?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net noncurrent DTL of $45,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Noncurrent DTA of $105,000 and noncurrent DTL of $150,000, shown separately

choices.label: C
choices.text: Net noncurrent DTA of $45,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Current DTA of $105,000 and noncurrent DTL of $150,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-10-45-6, deferred tax assets and liabilities within the same tax jurisdiction are offset and presented as a single net amount. All deferred taxes are classified as noncurrent per ASU 2015-17. Net amount = DTL $150,000 − DTA $63,000 − DTA $42,000 = DTL $150,000 − DTA $105,000 = net DTL of $45,000, presented as a single noncurrent liability.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-10-45-6により、同一課税管轄区域内のDTAとDTLは相殺して純額で表示する。ASU 2015-17により全て非流動。純額 = DTL $150,000 − DTA $105,000 = 純DTL $45,000を非流動負債として表示。
